title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
, India  

  

**Job Family Group:**

Research and Development  

  

**Worker Type:**

Regular  

  

**Posting Start Date:**

January 22, 2026  

  

**Business Unit:**

Finance  

  

**Experience Level:**

Experienced Professionals  

  

**Job Description:**

**What’s the role**

As a Senior Data Scientist within the Digital Process Twin (DPT) portfolio, you will lead the end\-to\-end technical design and build of process\-centric ML/AI solutions—owning runtime reliability and reusable assets as a individual contributor. You will apply process mining, optimization, and AI/ML techniques to process\-centric data to identify inefficiencies, predict process behavior, and optimize end\-to\-end business performance. You will collaborate closely with engineers, architects, and fellow data scientists to design scalable analytical solutions and integrate them into Shell’s process\-mining ecosystem.


The Digital Process Twin Centre of Excellence (CoE) aims to *“Empower Process Transformation through Digital Process Twins”* by advancing process\-mining and analytics capabilities. Shell is recognized as an industry thought leader in process intelligence, pioneering innovative analytical methodologies and data\-driven process transformation. The CoE collaborates with academic institutions, industry forums, and technology vendors to drive innovation and applied research in process analytics.


If you are passionate about building advanced analytical models using process event data and developing intelligent solutions that enhance operational agility and enable autonomous processes, we invite you to join us as a Senior Data Scientist driving Shell’s digital process transformation journey.


**What you’ll be doing**

* Own the technical architecture and reusable design patterns for analytical solutions — including discovery/ideation workshops with business stakeholders — from problem framing and data design to deployment and run operations.
* Define canonical process\-data models and event\-data standards; build versioned, reusable pipelines (with case IDs, activities, timestamps, attributes) from SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, etc.
* Apply process\-mining to discover patterns and root causes; engineer features from event logs and quantify improvement opportunities against target KPIs.
* Build and productionize ML/OR models on process event data (classification, anomaly detection, forecasting, prescriptive optimization) to enhance business\-process performance.
* Implement and enforce MLOps standards—CI/CD, versioning, automated tests (leakage/drift/fairness), model registry, and observability—with defined SLOs and rollback/playbooks; embed RAI/XAI by default, and ensure these standards are adopted consistently by Data Scientists across projects.
* Collaborate with data engineers and software developers to embed and scale models in Shell’s process\-mining platform for reliable, near\-real\-time analytics.
* Establish evaluation frameworks and a quality bar—offline benchmarks, shadow/canary runs, A/B or interleaving tests, and drift monitoring—tied to business KPIs (e.g., cycle time, STP%, backlog, rework).
Collaborate with academic and industry partners (vendors, startups, open\-source communities) to bring external perspectives, co\-develop prototypes, and translate research into production (PoCs benchmarks* deployment).
* Act as technical SME and raise the team’s technical bar via design reviews, code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions.

**What you bring**

8–10 years in applied data science/ML/OR, including* 2 years with process/event or behavioral data at enterprise scale.
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
End\-to\-end production delivery record: shipped* 3 production ML/OR solutions from design to stable run (CI/CD, versioning, observability) with defined Service Level Objectives (SLOs), demonstrating KPI uplift, and executing safe release/rollback procedures; embedded Responsible/Explainable AI (RAI/XAI).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR algorithms: hands\-on with supervised/unsupervised learning (classification, clustering), time\-series forecasting, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining methods, and prescriptive optimization/simulation; strong model selection \& trade\-off reasoning (features, evaluation, cost\-of\-error).
* Process\-data modeling \& standards: canonical event/process schemas, schema/versioning \& data\-quality standards (lineage, validation, freshness), and governed, reusable pipelines/feature sets; experience with schema evolution and late events.
* Platforms \& packaging: integrated models with Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) and open\-source tooling (e.g., PM4Py/ProM); packaged solutions as services/components adopted by other teams.
* Performance \& reliability: optimized cost/latency/throughput (profiling, vectorization, caching); built runbooks and dashboards that reduced Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) and prevented regressions.
Research production (with academia): led PoCs benchmarks* deployment with academic partners; reproduced state\-of\-the\-art (SOTA) results and translated them into maintainable production systems.
* Technical influence as a senior IC: raised the team’s technical bar via design/code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions; improved test coverage, reuse, and documentation.
* Rapid prototyping \& incubation: turn new ideas into quick, decision\-quality prototypes; design baselines, A/B or interleaving tests where relevant, and drive a build/kill/iterate path based on impact and cost.

**Must have Skills:**

* Python \& SQL, production\-grade code (tests, packaging, git).
* Process/event data modeling \& process mining (discovery, conformance, enhancement; features from event logs).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR: supervised/unsupervised, time\-series, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining, prescriptive optimization.
* MLOps in practice: CI/CD, containers, experiment tracking \& model registry, monitoring/observability with SLOs, safe release/rollback, RAI/XAI.
* Technical leadership as a senior IC: lead design/code reviews, mentor data scientists, co\-design with DE/PM/PO, run discovery/ideation workshops, and document decisions clearly.
* Cloud \& data platforms: Azure/AWS/GCP; Spark/Databricks (incl. jobs/orchestration exposure).
* Platform integration: embed models into Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) for near\-real\-time analytics.
* Performance engineering: profiling, vectorization, caching; cost/latency/throughput tuning.
* Tech transfer \& enablement: collaborate with academic/industry partners; coach via pair\-programming and coding exercises/workshops.
* Decision narratives for executives: translate experiments and model performance into clear recommendations with risks, trade\-offs, and expected impact; build decision\-ready dashboards/briefs.

**Nice have Skills:**

* Open\-source process\-mining \& feature/graph stores (PM4Py, ProM, Feast, Neo4j).
* Change leadership \& training: playbooks, brown\-bags, reusable templates that scale practices across teams.
* Community impact: publications/patents or meaningful open\-source contributions in process intelligence/applied AI.

**Key Competencies required for this role:**

* Applied Data Science Expertise
* Analytical Solution Architecture
* Technical Leadership

**What we offer**

You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet. You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another. You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself.


* Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together.
* Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead.
* Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices.
* Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise.
* Achieve your balance in a values\-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.
* Join an organisation working to become one of the most diverse and inclusive in the world. We strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and life experiences to apply.
* Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and
* international teams.
* Gain access to a wide range of training and development programmes.

**An Inclusive \& Progressive Place to Work**

* To power progress together, we need to attract and develop the brightest minds and make sure every voice is heard. Here are just some of the ways we’re nurturing an inclusive environment – one where you can express your ideas, extend your skills, and reach your potential.
* We’re creating a space where people with disabilities can excel through transparent recruitment process, workplace adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Feel free to let us know about your circumstances when you apply, and we’ll take it from there.
* We’re closing the gender gap – whether that’s through action on equal pay or by enabling more women to reach senior roles in engineering and technology.
* We’re striving to be a pioneer of an inclusive and diverse workplace, promoting equality for employees regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity.

**Shell in India**

Shell is a diversified energy company in India with 13,000 employees, and presence in Integrated Gas, Downstream, Power, Renewable and Upstream. Additionally, we have deep capabilities in R\&D, digitalisation, and business operations.


Our global strategy, Powering Progress, is designed to generate value for our shareholders, customers, and the wider society, and focuses on creating more value with less emissions. The strategy supports our purpose of providing more and cleaner energy solutions, with the aim of profitably transforming Shell into a net\-zero emissions energy business by 2050\. As India moves towards its target of net\-zero emissions by 2070, Shell India aims to play a leading role in securing vital energy for today, while investing in, and helping to build, the energy system of the future through strategic investments in the country.


Our Lubricants business serves over 50,000 consumers through a strong network of over 200 distributors, and operates an end\-to\-end value chain that spans conceptualization, development, and production at a world\-class blending plant at Taloja. Through our 350\-plus retail stations, we offer an integrated mobility experience including fuels, cafes, and convenience stores, with a prominent network of EV recharging facilities.


Shell owns and operates a LNG re\-gasification terminal at Hazira, Surat, with a capacity of 5 MTPA and a LNG truck\-loading unit that plays a crucial role in helping meet India’s growing demand for gas across sectors. In 2022, Shell acquired Sprng Energy in a $1\.55 billion deal to build an integrated energy transition business in the country. Sprng is a leading renewable energy company in India which develops and manages solar, wind, and hybrid power generation facilities and infrastructure.


Our three capability centres across Bangalore and Chennai serve as a technology and innovation powerhouse for Shell globally, working as a delivery engine for core technical, digital, and finance processes: pioneering digital innovation and cutting\-edge technologies across the energy sector. We also have strong academic partnerships and collaborations with leading universities and technology institutes to accelerate decarbonization efforts within the energy sector.


We are committed to positively contributing to the communities in which we operate through programmes on STEM Education, Skilling, and Livelihood across India. We nurture and invest in startups developing initiatives focused on accelerating energy innovation through programs such as Shell E4 and Shell Eco\-marathon. We also have strategic investments in new energy companies such as Husk Power, d.light, Orb Energy, and Cleantech Solar.


* \-

  

**DISCLAIMER:**

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Shell jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Shell/Shell Group companies around the world. The Shell Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Shell. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorised Shell representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand. Shell is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Innovation Engineer-Senior Software Engineer
description:
Founded in 1976, CGI is among the largest independent IT and business consulting services firms in the world. With 94,000  

consultants and professionals across the globe, CGI delivers an end\-to\-end portfolio of capabilities, from strategic IT and  

business consulting to systems integration, managed IT and business process services and intellectual property solutions. CGI  

works with clients through a local relationship model complemented by a global delivery network that helps clients digitally  

transform their organizations and accelerate results. CGI Fiscal 2024 reported revenue is CA$14\.68 billion and CGI shares are  

listed on the TSX (GIB.A) and the NYSE (GIB). Learn more at cgi.com.Key Requirements

**Your future duties and responsibilities**


Position: Senior Software Engineer  

Experience: 7\-12 years  

Category: Software Development/ Engineering  

Shift Timings: 1:00 pm to 10:00 pm  

Main location: Hyderabad  

Work Type: Work from office (All 5 Days work from office in ODC)  

Skill: AI/ML/Python/GenAI/GCP  

Employment Type: Full Time  

Job Id: J0126\-0735  

We are looking for a senior technologist with strong experience in cloud\-native product development and AI/GenAI platforms. The role involves driving innovation from concept to production while mentoring teams and partnering closely with business stakeholders.  

\#LI\-AD11

**Required qualifications to be successful in this role**


Key Responsibilities  

Lead end\-to\-end design and development of scalable, cloud\-native products from ideation, PoC, pilot to production rollout.  

  

Architect and implement distributed systems using microservices, APIs, containers (Docker), Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.  

  

Build, deploy, and optimize AI/ML and GenAI solutions, including LLM\-based applications, RAG pipelines, and vector databases.  

  

Ensure robust MLOps practices covering model lifecycle management, monitoring, versioning, and continuous improvement.  

  

Design systems adhering to 12\-factor app principles, focusing on high availability, autoscaling, resiliency, and cost optimization.  

  

Own cloud architecture across AWS/GCP/Azure, ensuring security, performance, and compliance with enterprise standards.  

  

Implement observability solutions for logging, monitoring, alerting, and performance tuning.  

  

Define and execute deployment strategies such as blue\-green and canary releases to ensure zero\-downtime deployments.  

  

Collaborate with product, business, and cross\-functional teams to translate business problems into scalable technical solutions.  

  

Mentor and guide engineers, set engineering best practices, and contribute to technical decision\-making in high\-ambiguity environments.  

  

Mandate Skills:  

7\+ years of hands\-on experience in product development, R\&D, or advanced engineering roles.  

  

Proven track record of delivering production\-grade systems, not limited to PoCs or prototypes.  

  

Strong expertise in cloud\-native architecture using AWS, GCP, or Azure.  

  

Hands\-on experience with Python, SQL, microservices, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD.  

  

Practical experience with AI/ML, GenAI, LLMs, RAG frameworks, vector databases, and MLOps.  

  

Strong understanding of cloud security, networking fundamentals, and best practices.  

  

Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively with technical and non\-technical stakeholders.  

  

Good\-to\-Have / Preferred Skills  

  

Experience designing enterprise\-scale AI platforms or internal developer platforms.  

  

Exposure to serverless architectures and event\-driven systems.  

  

Experience optimizing cloud costs and performance at scale.  

  

Prior experience in mentoring, technical leadership, or architecture review boards.  

  

Familiarity with open\-source AI/ML frameworks and tooling.  

  

Experience working in fast\-paced, high\-growth, or ambiguous environments.  

  

Publications, patents, or contributions to open\-source projects are a plus.

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**  

  

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…  

  

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.  

  

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.  

  

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well\-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
  

  

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Senior Software Engineer - AI/ML
description:
### **Senior Software Engineer \- AI/ML**

* JR\-157602
* Hybrid
* Bengaluru
* Technology
* Full time

**Who are we?**



Equinix is the world’s digital infrastructure company®, shortening the path to connectivity to enable the innovations that enrich our work, life and planet.

  


A place where bold ideas are welcomed, human connection is valued, and everyone has the opportunity to shape their future.



Help us challenge assumptions, uncover bias, and remove barriers—because progress starts with fresh ideas. You’ll find belonging, purpose, and a team that welcomes you—because when you feel valued, you’re empowered to do your best work.**Job Summary**


Equinix is seeking a highly motivated Senior Software Engineer with a strong focus on building AI/ML‑driven solutions to join our Bangalore team. In this role, you will design, build, and launch production‑grade, multi‑agent systems and conversational experiences, spanning orchestration, RAG design, model serving, real‑time inference, and observability. You will work with state‑of‑the‑art Large Language Models (LLMs), agentic AI frameworks, and modern engineering practices to deliver solutions that meet real customer and business needs.


As a Senior Software Engineer, you will own the AI roadmap, serve as the primary technical subject‑matter expert, and collaborate closely with IT, security, and business teams to deliver secure, compliant, and highly scalable AI solutions. This role offers the opportunity to shape how AI is adopted and operationalized across the organization.


This position is ideal for someone passionate about applying cutting‑edge AI technologies (such as GPT, Claude, and LLaMA) to real‑world problems, with strong engineering fundamentals and product intuition. You will work at the intersection of AI reasoning, automation, and user experience, building intelligent agents capable of decision‑making, context retention, and multi‑step task execution.

**Responsibilities**

* Design, build, and own production‑grade Agentic AI systems using frameworks such as OpenAI, LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, CrewAI, Google ADK, or similar platforms, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and maintainability
* Architect and develop conversational AI assistants and intelligent agents across multiple channels (chat, APIs, embedded applications), ensuring seamless user experiences and enterprise readiness
* Integrate Large Language Models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.) with APIs, databases, vector stores, and enterprise systems to enable end‑to‑end AI‑driven workflows
* Design and implement memory, retrieval, planning, and reasoning components to build goal‑oriented, context‑aware agents capable of multi‑step decision making.
* Apply and optimize advanced prompting strategies (prompt engineering, chain‑of‑thought reasoning, tool use, and agent orchestration) to improve accuracy, reliability, latency, and cost efficiency
* Lead the design and adoption of RAG (Retrieval‑Augmented Generation) architectures, ensuring high factual accuracy, relevance, and traceability across enterprise knowledge sources
* Establish evaluation frameworks for AI systems, including offline and online metrics, hallucination detection, regression testing, guardrails, and human‑in‑the‑loop feedback mechanisms
* Operationalize AI systems in production, including monitoring, logging, observability, cost controls, and continuous improvement using feedback loops and performance analytics
* Build and maintain LLMOps / MLOps pipelines, covering model versioning, experimentation, deployment, rollback strategies, and environment promotion
* Ensure security, privacy, and ethical AI practices by implementing data protection, prompt and output safeguards, policy enforcement, and compliance with enterprise standards
* Collaborate closely with Product Managers, business stakeholders, and cross‑functional teams to translate business objectives into AI‑driven solutions with measurable impact
* Conduct feasibility assessments and develop proofs‑of‑concept, validating emerging AI approaches, tools, and architectures before production adoption
* Stay ahead of emerging AI technologies and trends, proactively identifying opportunities for automation, efficiency gains, and AI‑powered enhancements
* Own technical decision‑making and architecture tradeoffs, balancing performance, scalability, security, and cost in enterprise environments
* Mentor junior engineers and guide technical best practices, while educating non‑technical stakeholders on AI capabilities, limitations, and responsible use.
* Contribute to engineering excellence by defining standards, documenting architectures, and fostering a collaborative, high‑performance team culture

**Qualifications**


Required

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
* 5\+ years of strong software engineering experience with proficiency in Python and familiarity with JavaScript / TypeScript or similar languages
* 2\+ years of hands‑on experience building solutions using Agentic AI and LLM frameworks such as OpenAI, Hugging Face, LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, CrewAI, or Semantic Kernel
* Strong understanding of software engineering best practices, including version control, CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and code review
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and modern deployment practices, including Docker and Kubernetes
* Proficiency working with vector databases and embedding systems such as Pinecone, FAISS, Weaviate, Chroma, or Milvus
* Demonstrated experience building, deploying, and maintaining generative AI applications in production environments
* Solid background in NLP, machine learning, including model training, fine‑tuning, or evaluation techniques
* Experience designing and implementing multi‑agent systems, autonomous workflows, or AI‑driven orchestration pipelines
* Understanding of LLM performance considerations, including latency, cost optimization, prompt reliability, and evaluation
* Awareness of AI security, data privacy, and responsible AI principles in enterprise environments
* Ability to work effectively across cross‑functional teams and global time zones, with strong communication skills and the ability to influence both technical and non‑technical stakeholders
* Proven ability to drive technical decisions, mentor peers, and contribute to a high‑performing engineering culture


How You will find success

* Cultivate a strong sense of curiosity and a product\-focused mindset
* Collaborate effectively with managers, cross\-functional teams, and teammates to achieve shared goals
* Demonstrate persistence, creativity, and an unwavering drive to deliver results
* Take pride in ownership and consistently strive for excellence in every initiative
* Apply an analytical approach to tackle complex problems, identify root causes, and develop data\-driven, impactful solutions


Equinix is committed to ensuring that our employment process is open to all individuals, including those with a disability. If you are a qualified candidate and need assistance or an accommodation, please let us know by completing form.


Equinix is an Equal Employment Opportunity and, in the U.S., an Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to unlawful consideration of race, color, religion, creed, national or ethnic origin, ancestry, place of birth, citizenship, sex, pregnancy / childbirth or related medical conditions, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or domestic partnership status, age, veteran or military status, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, political / organizational affiliation, status as a victim or family member of a victim of crime or abuse, or any other status protected by applicable law.

**We use artificial intelligence in our hiring process. Learn more** **here****.**

description:
, India

Research and Development

Regular

January 22, 2026

Finance

Experienced Professionals  

  

**Job Description:**

**What’s the role**

As a Senior Data Scientist within the Digital Process Twin (DPT) portfolio, you will lead the end\-to\-end technical design and build of process\-centric ML/AI solutions—owning runtime reliability and reusable assets as a individual contributor. You will apply process mining, optimization, and AI/ML techniques to process\-centric data to identify inefficiencies, predict process behavior, and optimize end\-to\-end business performance. You will collaborate closely with engineers, architects, and fellow data scientists to design scalable analytical solutions and integrate them into Shell’s process\-mining ecosystem.


The Digital Process Twin Centre of Excellence (CoE) aims to *“Empower Process Transformation through Digital Process Twins”* by advancing process\-mining and analytics capabilities. Shell is recognized as an industry thought leader in process intelligence, pioneering innovative analytical methodologies and data\-driven process transformation. The CoE collaborates with academic institutions, industry forums, and technology vendors to drive innovation and applied research in process analytics.


If you are passionate about building advanced analytical models using process event data and developing intelligent solutions that enhance operational agility and enable autonomous processes, we invite you to join us as a Senior Data Scientist driving Shell’s digital process transformation journey.


**What you’ll be doing**

* Own the technical architecture and reusable design patterns for analytical solutions — including discovery/ideation workshops with business stakeholders — from problem framing and data design to deployment and run operations.
* Define canonical process\-data models and event\-data standards; build versioned, reusable pipelines (with case IDs, activities, timestamps, attributes) from SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, etc.
* Apply process\-mining to discover patterns and root causes; engineer features from event logs and quantify improvement opportunities against target KPIs.
* Build and productionize ML/OR models on process event data (classification, anomaly detection, forecasting, prescriptive optimization) to enhance business\-process performance.
* Implement and enforce MLOps standards—CI/CD, versioning, automated tests (leakage/drift/fairness), model registry, and observability—with defined SLOs and rollback/playbooks; embed RAI/XAI by default, and ensure these standards are adopted consistently by Data Scientists across projects.
* Collaborate with data engineers and software developers to embed and scale models in Shell’s process\-mining platform for reliable, near\-real\-time analytics.
* Establish evaluation frameworks and a quality bar—offline benchmarks, shadow/canary runs, A/B or interleaving tests, and drift monitoring—tied to business KPIs (e.g., cycle time, STP%, backlog, rework).
Collaborate with academic and industry partners (vendors, startups, open\-source communities) to bring external perspectives, co\-develop prototypes, and translate research into production (PoCs benchmarks* deployment).
* Act as technical SME and raise the team’s technical bar via design reviews, code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions.

**What you bring**

8–10 years in applied data science/ML/OR, including* 2 years with process/event or behavioral data at enterprise scale.
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
End\-to\-end production delivery record: shipped* 3 production ML/OR solutions from design to stable run (CI/CD, versioning, observability) with defined Service Level Objectives (SLOs), demonstrating KPI uplift, and executing safe release/rollback procedures; embedded Responsible/Explainable AI (RAI/XAI).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR algorithms: hands\-on with supervised/unsupervised learning (classification, clustering), time\-series forecasting, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining methods, and prescriptive optimization/simulation; strong model selection \& trade\-off reasoning (features, evaluation, cost\-of\-error).
* Process\-data modeling \& standards: canonical event/process schemas, schema/versioning \& data\-quality standards (lineage, validation, freshness), and governed, reusable pipelines/feature sets; experience with schema evolution and late events.
* Platforms \& packaging: integrated models with Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) and open\-source tooling (e.g., PM4Py/ProM); packaged solutions as services/components adopted by other teams.
* Performance \& reliability: optimized cost/latency/throughput (profiling, vectorization, caching); built runbooks and dashboards that reduced Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) and prevented regressions.
Research production (with academia): led PoCs benchmarks* deployment with academic partners; reproduced state\-of\-the\-art (SOTA) results and translated them into maintainable production systems.
* Technical influence as a senior IC: raised the team’s technical bar via design/code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions; improved test coverage, reuse, and documentation.
* Rapid prototyping \& incubation: turn new ideas into quick, decision\-quality prototypes; design baselines, A/B or interleaving tests where relevant, and drive a build/kill/iterate path based on impact and cost.

**Must have Skills:**

* Python \& SQL, production\-grade code (tests, packaging, git).
* Process/event data modeling \& process mining (discovery, conformance, enhancement; features from event logs).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR: supervised/unsupervised, time\-series, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining, prescriptive optimization.
* MLOps in practice: CI/CD, containers, experiment tracking \& model registry, monitoring/observability with SLOs, safe release/rollback, RAI/XAI.
* Technical leadership as a senior IC: lead design/code reviews, mentor data scientists, co\-design with DE/PM/PO, run discovery/ideation workshops, and document decisions clearly.
* Cloud \& data platforms: Azure/AWS/GCP; Spark/Databricks (incl. jobs/orchestration exposure).
* Platform integration: embed models into Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) for near\-real\-time analytics.
* Performance engineering: profiling, vectorization, caching; cost/latency/throughput tuning.
* Tech transfer \& enablement: collaborate with academic/industry partners; coach via pair\-programming and coding exercises/workshops.
* Decision narratives for executives: translate experiments and model performance into clear recommendations with risks, trade\-offs, and expected impact; build decision\-ready dashboards/briefs.

**Nice have Skills:**

* Open\-source process\-mining \& feature/graph stores (PM4Py, ProM, Feast, Neo4j).
* Change leadership \& training: playbooks, brown\-bags, reusable templates that scale practices across teams.
* Community impact: publications/patents or meaningful open\-source contributions in process intelligence/applied AI.

**Key Competencies required for this role:**

* Applied Data Science Expertise
* Analytical Solution Architecture
* Technical Leadership

**What we offer**

You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet. You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another. You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself.


* Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together.
* Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead.
* Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices.
* Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise.
* Achieve your balance in a values\-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.
* Join an organisation working to become one of the most diverse and inclusive in the world. We strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and life experiences to apply.
* Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and
* international teams.
* Gain access to a wide range of training and development programmes.

**An Inclusive \& Progressive Place to Work**

* To power progress together, we need to attract and develop the brightest minds and make sure every voice is heard. Here are just some of the ways we’re nurturing an inclusive environment – one where you can express your ideas, extend your skills, and reach your potential.
* We’re creating a space where people with disabilities can excel through transparent recruitment process, workplace adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Feel free to let us know about your circumstances when you apply, and we’ll take it from there.
* We’re closing the gender gap – whether that’s through action on equal pay or by enabling more women to reach senior roles in engineering and technology.
* We’re striving to be a pioneer of an inclusive and diverse workplace, promoting equality for employees regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity.

**Shell in India**

Shell is a diversified energy company in India with 13,000 employees, and presence in Integrated Gas, Downstream, Power, Renewable and Upstream. Additionally, we have deep capabilities in R\&D, digitalisation, and business operations.


Our global strategy, Powering Progress, is designed to generate value for our shareholders, customers, and the wider society, and focuses on creating more value with less emissions. The strategy supports our purpose of providing more and cleaner energy solutions, with the aim of profitably transforming Shell into a net\-zero emissions energy business by 2050\. As India moves towards its target of net\-zero emissions by 2070, Shell India aims to play a leading role in securing vital energy for today, while investing in, and helping to build, the energy system of the future through strategic investments in the country.


Our Lubricants business serves over 50,000 consumers through a strong network of over 200 distributors, and operates an end\-to\-end value chain that spans conceptualization, development, and production at a world\-class blending plant at Taloja. Through our 350\-plus retail stations, we offer an integrated mobility experience including fuels, cafes, and convenience stores, with a prominent network of EV recharging facilities.


Shell owns and operates a LNG re\-gasification terminal at Hazira, Surat, with a capacity of 5 MTPA and a LNG truck\-loading unit that plays a crucial role in helping meet India’s growing demand for gas across sectors. In 2022, Shell acquired Sprng Energy in a $1\.55 billion deal to build an integrated energy transition business in the country. Sprng is a leading renewable energy company in India which develops and manages solar, wind, and hybrid power generation facilities and infrastructure.


Our three capability centres across Bangalore and Chennai serve as a technology and innovation powerhouse for Shell globally, working as a delivery engine for core technical, digital, and finance processes: pioneering digital innovation and cutting\-edge technologies across the energy sector. We also have strong academic partnerships and collaborations with leading universities and technology institutes to accelerate decarbonization efforts within the energy sector.


We are committed to positively contributing to the communities in which we operate through programmes on STEM Education, Skilling, and Livelihood across India. We nurture and invest in startups developing initiatives focused on accelerating energy innovation through programs such as Shell E4 and Shell Eco\-marathon. We also have strategic investments in new energy companies such as Husk Power, d.light, Orb Energy, and Cleantech Solar.


* \-

Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorised Shell representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand. Shell is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

description:
, India

**Worker Type:**

Regular

January 22, 2026  

  

**Business Unit:**

Finance  

  

**Experience Level:**

Experienced Professionals  

  

**Job Description:**

**What’s the role**

As a Senior Data Scientist within the Digital Process Twin (DPT) portfolio, you will lead the end\-to\-end technical design and build of process\-centric ML/AI solutions—owning runtime reliability and reusable assets as a individual contributor. You will apply process mining, optimization, and AI/ML techniques to process\-centric data to identify inefficiencies, predict process behavior, and optimize end\-to\-end business performance. You will collaborate closely with engineers, architects, and fellow data scientists to design scalable analytical solutions and integrate them into Shell’s process\-mining ecosystem.


The Digital Process Twin Centre of Excellence (CoE) aims to *“Empower Process Transformation through Digital Process Twins”* by advancing process\-mining and analytics capabilities. Shell is recognized as an industry thought leader in process intelligence, pioneering innovative analytical methodologies and data\-driven process transformation. The CoE collaborates with academic institutions, industry forums, and technology vendors to drive innovation and applied research in process analytics.


If you are passionate about building advanced analytical models using process event data and developing intelligent solutions that enhance operational agility and enable autonomous processes, we invite you to join us as a Senior Data Scientist driving Shell’s digital process transformation journey.


**What you’ll be doing**

* Own the technical architecture and reusable design patterns for analytical solutions — including discovery/ideation workshops with business stakeholders — from problem framing and data design to deployment and run operations.
* Define canonical process\-data models and event\-data standards; build versioned, reusable pipelines (with case IDs, activities, timestamps, attributes) from SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, etc.
* Apply process\-mining to discover patterns and root causes; engineer features from event logs and quantify improvement opportunities against target KPIs.
* Build and productionize ML/OR models on process event data (classification, anomaly detection, forecasting, prescriptive optimization) to enhance business\-process performance.
* Implement and enforce MLOps standards—CI/CD, versioning, automated tests (leakage/drift/fairness), model registry, and observability—with defined SLOs and rollback/playbooks; embed RAI/XAI by default, and ensure these standards are adopted consistently by Data Scientists across projects.
* Collaborate with data engineers and software developers to embed and scale models in Shell’s process\-mining platform for reliable, near\-real\-time analytics.
* Establish evaluation frameworks and a quality bar—offline benchmarks, shadow/canary runs, A/B or interleaving tests, and drift monitoring—tied to business KPIs (e.g., cycle time, STP%, backlog, rework).
Collaborate with academic and industry partners (vendors, startups, open\-source communities) to bring external perspectives, co\-develop prototypes, and translate research into production (PoCs benchmarks* deployment).
* Act as technical SME and raise the team’s technical bar via design reviews, code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions.

**What you bring**

8–10 years in applied data science/ML/OR, including* 2 years with process/event or behavioral data at enterprise scale.
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
End\-to\-end production delivery record: shipped* 3 production ML/OR solutions from design to stable run (CI/CD, versioning, observability) with defined Service Level Objectives (SLOs), demonstrating KPI uplift, and executing safe release/rollback procedures; embedded Responsible/Explainable AI (RAI/XAI).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR algorithms: hands\-on with supervised/unsupervised learning (classification, clustering), time\-series forecasting, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining methods, and prescriptive optimization/simulation; strong model selection \& trade\-off reasoning (features, evaluation, cost\-of\-error).
* Process\-data modeling \& standards: canonical event/process schemas, schema/versioning \& data\-quality standards (lineage, validation, freshness), and governed, reusable pipelines/feature sets; experience with schema evolution and late events.
* Platforms \& packaging: integrated models with Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) and open\-source tooling (e.g., PM4Py/ProM); packaged solutions as services/components adopted by other teams.
* Performance \& reliability: optimized cost/latency/throughput (profiling, vectorization, caching); built runbooks and dashboards that reduced Mean Time To Recovery (MTTR) and prevented regressions.
Research production (with academia): led PoCs benchmarks* deployment with academic partners; reproduced state\-of\-the\-art (SOTA) results and translated them into maintainable production systems.
* Technical influence as a senior IC: raised the team’s technical bar via design/code reviews, pair\-programming, coding exercises/workshops, and learning sessions; improved test coverage, reuse, and documentation.
* Rapid prototyping \& incubation: turn new ideas into quick, decision\-quality prototypes; design baselines, A/B or interleaving tests where relevant, and drive a build/kill/iterate path based on impact and cost.

**Must have Skills:**

* Python \& SQL, production\-grade code (tests, packaging, git).
* Process/event data modeling \& process mining (discovery, conformance, enhancement; features from event logs).
* Applied ML/AI \& OR: supervised/unsupervised, time\-series, anomaly detection, graph/process\-mining, prescriptive optimization.
* MLOps in practice: CI/CD, containers, experiment tracking \& model registry, monitoring/observability with SLOs, safe release/rollback, RAI/XAI.
* Technical leadership as a senior IC: lead design/code reviews, mentor data scientists, co\-design with DE/PM/PO, run discovery/ideation workshops, and document decisions clearly.
* Cloud \& data platforms: Azure/AWS/GCP; Spark/Databricks (incl. jobs/orchestration exposure).
* Platform integration: embed models into Celonis/Signavio (or equivalent) for near\-real\-time analytics.
* Performance engineering: profiling, vectorization, caching; cost/latency/throughput tuning.
* Tech transfer \& enablement: collaborate with academic/industry partners; coach via pair\-programming and coding exercises/workshops.
* Decision narratives for executives: translate experiments and model performance into clear recommendations with risks, trade\-offs, and expected impact; build decision\-ready dashboards/briefs.

**Nice have Skills:**

* Open\-source process\-mining \& feature/graph stores (PM4Py, ProM, Feast, Neo4j).
* Change leadership \& training: playbooks, brown\-bags, reusable templates that scale practices across teams.
* Community impact: publications/patents or meaningful open\-source contributions in process intelligence/applied AI.

**Key Competencies required for this role:**

* Applied Data Science Expertise
* Analytical Solution Architecture
* Technical Leadership

title: Senior Manager - Platform & Architecture
description:
**About the role:**
-------------------


As a Sr. Manager, Platform \& Architecture you will lead the definition, governance \& continuous evolution of the enterprise data architecture vision and roadmap, ensuring strong alignment with business strategy and digital transformation goals. This role is accountable for establishing modern, secure, and scalable data platform architectures that enable advanced analytics, AI/ML, generative AI, and self service data capabilities across the organization.


You will provide strategic leadership in embedding AI into the enterprise data platform, enabling use cases such as predictive analytics, intelligent automation, and generative AI, while ensuring responsible AI practices, model governance, data quality, and regulatory compliance.


The role requires deep expertise in data architecture, cloud data platforms, analytics ecosystems, and platform security, combined with strong people and stakeholder management skills. You will guide the evaluation and adoption of emerging technologies, oversee PoC initiatives, and drive the operationalization of AI/ML and GenAI solutions.


Working closely with data engineering, analytics, AI/ML, security, and business leaders, you will ensure the delivery of trusted, high performing, and future ready data and AI platforms that scale with organizational needs.


**What you'll do:**


### **Strategic Leadership \& Architecture Vision**


* Define and own the enterprise data architecture vision and roadmap aligned with business strategy.
* Establish architectural principles, standards, and best practices across data modeling, integration, metadata, and analytics
* Guide architectural decisions to support scalability, performance, security, and future growth.
* Evaluate and approve new data technologies, platforms, and tools


### **Team Management \& Development**


* Lead, mentor, and manage data modelers \& architects, platform engineers and data engineers
* Set goals, conduct performance reviews, and support career growth and skills development
* Ensure effective workload planning, prioritization, and resource allocation
* Promote a culture of collaboration, documentation, and architectural discipline


### **Data Platform \& Architecture Design**


* Design and oversee end to end data architectures, including ingestion, storage, processing, analytics, and consumption layers
* Define scalable patterns for batch, near real time, and streaming data processing
* Establish standards for data platforms, ingestion frameworks, transformation and modeling approaches, and BI/visualization tools
* Ensure platform reliability, performance, scalability, and cost optimization


### **Platform Engineering, Security \& Governance**


* Architect platforms with security by design, including IAM, RBAC, data encryption, masking, and auditing
* Collaborate with security and compliance teams to meet regulatory and data privacy requirements
* Define and enforce data governance standards, including metadata management, lineage, data quality, and observability
* Support platform monitoring, availability, and operational excellence


### **Advanced Analytics \& Innovation**


* Enable advanced analytics use cases, including forecasting, predictive modeling, and statistical analysis
* Support data science and analytics teams by designing architectures for ML pipelines, feature stores, and model deployment
* Lead Proof of Concept (PoC) initiatives to evaluate new data, analytics, and platform tools
* Assess emerging technologies for scalability, security, integration, and business value


### **Self\-Service \& Data Enablement**


* Drive the self\-service analytics strategy to empower analysts and business users
* Design curated, governed data layers and semantic models for trusted self\-service access
* Reduce dependency on IT by enabling reusable data assets and standardized analytics frameworks
* Partner with stakeholders to align data products with business needs


**Experience you'll need:**
---------------------------


* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or related field
* 10 \- 15\+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics, or platform architecture roles
* Strong experience designing and implementing modern data architectures and cloud data platforms.
* Hands\-on and architectural experience with:
	+ **Snowflake** or similar cloud data warehouse/data lake experience
	+ **Tableau** or similar BI tools
	+ **Fivetran** or equivalent ingestion tools
	+ **dbt** or similar data transformation frameworks
* Proficiency in **SQL, Python, Spark**, and data modeling techniques.
* Experience with cloud platforms such as **AWS, Azure, or GCP**


**Preferred Qualifications:**
-----------------------------


* Experience with advanced analytics, forecasting, and predictive modeling.

title: Senior MLOps Engineer
description:
**Company Description** **Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited** is a 100% owned subsidiary of Robert Bosch GmbH, one of the world's leading global supplier of technology and services, offering end\-to\-end Engineering, IT and Business Solutions. With over 27,000\+ associates, it’s the largest software development center of Bosch, outside Germany, indicating that it is the Technology Powerhouse of Bosch in India with a global footprint and presence in the US, Europe and the Asia Pacific region.

 **Job Description**  

As a Senior MLOps Engineer in the Perception team, you will play a pivotal role in building and maintaining the backbone of our L2\+ ADAS stack. This senior role calls for an experienced engineer who can think critically, execute independently, and deliver results on scalable deep learning infrastructure, optimize massive data ingestion pipelines, and ensure maximum efficiency across our compute clusters.


You will be responsible for the entire DL infrastructure lifecycle—from managing Azure storage and hybrid Kubernetes clusters to designing efficient data loaders for multimodal training. You will work at the intersection of infrastructure, data engineering, and deep learning, enabling feature teams to train complex models (single frame, temporal, and multimodal) with speed and reliability. Your ability to solve abstract infrastructure challenges and apply "T\-shaped" expertise—going deep in areas like infrastructure, multitask deep learning among others while maintaining breadth in software design—will be key to our success.


Responsibilities


Deep Learning Infrastructure \& Compute:  

* Manage and optimize the entire DL infrastructure, including Azure Blob Storage integration, VNET setups, and hybrid compute resources (Cloud and On\-premise/Frankfurt clusters).
* Lead performance investigations and benchmarking for next\-gen hardware (e.g., comparing H200 vs. H100, Azure native vs. deployment nodes) to ensure cost and speed efficiency.
* Maintain and scale Kubernetes clusters for training and inference workloads.

Data Pipelines \& Efficient Loading:  

* Architect and develop high\-performance data loaders for complex multimodal datasets (camera, radar, temporal/non\-temporal data).
* Modernize data processing pipelines using Ray and Kubernetes to parallelize data caching, shuffling, and oversampling.
* Leverage PyArrow and SQL to optimize data consumption and integration with data loops.
* Implement efficient dataset update strategies (handling deltas) and ensure seamless integration of new tasks into the multimodal multi\-task network.

CI/CD, Monitoring \& Quality:  

* Design and maintain robust GitHub Workflows and CI pipelines for new and existing feature teams.
* Develop KPI dashboards using Grafana to monitor compute usage, GPU efficiency, unit test durations, and overall system health.
* Manage dependency updates (Torch upgrades, Ubuntu updates, Hydra maintenance, Dependabots) to ensure a secure and modern stack.
* Drive software design excellence by performing thorough code reviews (PRs) and enforcing high standards in software architecture.


Embedded \& Evaluation:  

* Establish scalable evaluation pipelines for embedded targets, specifically for QNN boards and other edge devices.
* Collaborate with feature teams to support model compression experiments and on\-target performance verification.

Required Qualifications  

* Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. An advanced degree is an advantage.
* Experience: 5\+ years of industry experience in MLOps, Data Engineering, or Software Infrastructure, with a focus on Deep Learning systems.
* Programming \& Software Design: Expert\-level proficiency in Python with a strong emphasis on clean software design, object\-oriented programming, and architectural patterns.
* Infrastructure \& Orchestration: Deep hands\-on experience with Kubernetes, Docker, and Cloud platforms (specifically Azure ML, Azure Storage/Networking).
* Big Data \& Optimization: Proficiency with high\-performance data processing tools such as Ray, PyArrow, and SQL. Experience optimizing data loading bottlenecks for


GPU training.  

* DevOps \& Monitoring: Experience setting up complex CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions) and observability stacks (Grafana, Prometheus).
* Soft Skills: Strong problem\-solving abilities, proactiveness, and ownership of complex topics. Ability to adapt quickly to new technologies and work collaboratively in a supportive, high\-performance team.
 **Qualifications**  

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. An advanced degree is an advantage.

 **Additional Information**  

* Deep Learning Knowledge: While deep algorithm knowledge is not mandatory, a strong understanding of Deep Learning workflows (PyTorch, training loops, multi\-task learning) is highly beneficial.
* Embedded Systems: Experience with deploying or evaluating models on embedded hardware (Qualcomm QNN, etc.) or setting up hardware\-in\-the\-loop pipelines.
* T\-Shaped Skills: Ability to specialize deeply in infrastructure while lending a hand to feature teams on diverse topics (e.g., documentation builds, visualization tools).

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description:
**Job Summary**

We are looking for a data\-driven **Machine Learning Engineer** to join our AI team. In this role, you will bridge the gap between data science and software engineering by building, deploying, and maintaining scalable machine learning models. As a junior member of the team, you will work closely with senior engineers to transform raw data into intelligent features and predictive systems. This is an ideal position for a recent graduate or early\-career professional who is passionate about AI and eager to solve real\-world problems through automation and predictive analytics.

**Key Responsibilities**

· **Model Development \& Training:** Assist in the design and implementation of ML models (Supervised, Unsupervised, and Deep Learning) using frameworks like PyTorch or TensorFlow.

· **Data Preprocessing:** Build and maintain data pipelines to clean, normalize, and transform raw data into high\-quality features for model training.

· **Model Evaluation:** Use statistical metrics (Accuracy, Precision/Recall, F1\-score, RMSE) to evaluate model performance and perform hyperparameter tuning to optimize results.

· **Deployment (MLOps):** Collaborate with the engineering team to containerize models (Docker) and deploy them into production environments (AWS/Azure/GCP).

· **Monitoring \& Maintenance:** Monitor deployed models for performance degradation (data drift) and perform periodic retraining to ensure continued accuracy.

· **Research \& Documentation:** Stay updated with the latest AI research and document the development lifecycle, from experiment results to production workflows.

· **Cross\-functional Collaboration:** Work with Data Scientists to understand business requirements and with Software Engineers to integrate ML models into end\-user applications.

**Required Skills \& Qualifications**

**Programming \& Software**

* Python: Mastery of OOP, data structures, and efficient scripting.
* SQL: Writing queries to extract and join data from relational databases.
* Git: Version control, branching, and collaborative workflows.
* Linux/Bash: Basic command\-line proficiency for server management.

**Machine Learning Core**

* Classical ML: Proficiency in Scikit\-learn (Regression, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost).
* Deep Learning: Basic experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow (Neural Networks, CNNs).
* Data Libraries: High proficiency in Pandas (cleaning) and NumPy (computation).
* Evaluation: Knowledge of metrics like Precision\-Recall, F1\-Score, and AUC\-ROC.

**Mathematics \& Statistics**

* Linear Algebra: Understanding matrix operations and vectorization.
* Statistics: Probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and p\-values.
* Calculus: Understanding Gradient Descent and loss optimization.

Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Fresher

Pay: ₹120,000\.00 \- ₹350,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

title: Senior AI Infrastructure Architect
description:
Innovatia Technical Services Inc. (ITSI) is an industry\-leading technology outsourcing support provider with expertise in the telecommunications industry. Operating for over 20 years, we specialize in workflow and knowledge processes, as well as technical support, helpdesk and multilingual support services. With over 300 professional experts across the globe, we service some of the worlds' most successful Fortune 500 and Fortune 1000 companies.  


Key Responsibilities  


Architect the Future: Lead the end\-to\-end design and development of our AI infrastructure, encompassing hardware, software, networking, and multi\-cloud environments  


Innovate and Evaluate: Assess, select, and implement the best\-in\-class technologies, tools, and frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, Kubernetes, Docker) to build and maintain our AI platforms.  


Optimize for Performance: Engineer and implement infrastructure that scales seamlessly with our evolving AI/ML needs, continuously monitoring and optimizing for both performance and cost\-efficiency.  


Champion Security and Compliance: Define and enforce infrastructure standards and best practices, ensuring unwavering compliance with security policies, data protection regulations, and ethical AI principles.  


Build Data\-Driven Pipelines: Collaborate on the architecture and implementation of highly efficient data pipelines for our AI models, from ingestion and storage to processing and management.  


Lead and Inspire: Provide technical leadership and mentorship to cross\-functional teams, fostering a culture of excellence and best practices in AI infrastructure.  


Solve Complex Challenges: Diagnose and resolve intricate infrastructure issues, ensuring the high availability and reliability of all our AI systems.  


Stay Ahead of the Curve: Keep your finger on the pulse of the latest advancements in AI, machine learning, and cloud computing, driving innovation within the organization.  


Document for Success: Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for all AI infrastructure designs, implementations, and operational procedures.  


Key Skills \& Experience Required  


Education: A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.  


Experience:  


* 15\+ years of experience in infrastructure architecture.
* A minimum of 3\-5 years of dedicated experience in designing and building AI\-specific infrastructure.
* Proven success in deploying scalable and secure AI solutions in cloud environments
* Extensive, hands\-on experience with containerization and orchestration technologies like Docker and Kubernetes.

Technical Prowess:  


* Proficiency with the command line and experience with both cloud\-native and on\-premise data center deployments.
* A strong understanding of deep learning architectures and the latest developments in Large Language Models (LLMs).
* Expertise in NVIDIA hardware and software, including performance tuning and diagnostics.
* Hands\-on experience with GPU systems, including performance testing, tuning, and benchmarking.
* Proficiency in programming languages such as Python.
* A deep understanding of cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) and cloud\-native architectures.
* In\-depth knowledge of networking, storage, and security best practices in a cloud context.
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or CloudFormation.
* Familiarity with DevOps and MLOps principles and practices.
* Soft Skills:
* Exceptional problem\-solving and analytical abilities with a data\-driven approach.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, capable of articulating complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.
* Proven ability to lead, mentor, and collaborate effectively within a team environment.
* A strategic mindset with the ability to align technical solutions with overarching business goals.
* A proactive, adaptable, and continuous learner who thrives in a dynamic technological landscape.

Benefits at Innovatia  


* Comprehensive Health Insurance policy
* Employee Wellness Program with focus on mental health
* Robust reward and recognition programs
* Company incentive programs offered
* Attractive leave policy: Holiday Leave, Maternity Leave, Paternity Leave, Birthday leave, Bereavement Leave and Paid Leave for personal time off
* Ample growth and learning opportunities
* Remote work opportunities
* Focus on work/life balance
* Immigration Program supporting immigration to Canada for eligible employees

We thank all candidates for their interest, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.  
Innovatia is an equal opportunity employer.

title: AI/ML Engineer Intern
description:
**Who We Are:**



CrashPlan® provides cyber\-ready data resilience and governance in a single platform for organizations whose ideas power their revenue. With its comprehensive backup and recovery capabilities for data stored on servers, on endpoint devices, and in SaaS applications, CrashPlan's solutions are trusted by entrepreneurs, professionals, and businesses of all sizes worldwide. From ransomware recovery and breaches to migrations and legal holds, CrashPlan's suite of products ensures the safety and compliance of your data without disruption.



**What You Will Be Doing:**

We are looking for an Intern AI/ML Engineer to join our specialized engineering team. In this high\-impact role, you will contribute to the development and implementation of core AI/ML capabilities that will enhance our data protection platform's intelligence and automation. You will work closely with Senior Engineers to gain hands\-on experience contributing to real production systems used by customers globally, focusing on how AI can drive operational efficiency and advanced cyber\-defense features.



This role is ideal for fresh graduates or students looking to specialize in applied machine learning, data security, and model development in a SaaS environment.


**Day in the Life:**


* Work as part of a dedicated AI/ML scrum team, learning MLOps and secure development best practices
* Assist in the design and development of data pipelines for internal data analysis and security\-related insights
* Contribute to implementing and testing machine learning models focused on problem domains like anomaly detection, log analysis, and data simulation
* Help set up the foundational infrastructure for deploying and monitoring AI/ML features in a cloud environment
* Participate in code reviews, technical design discussions, and sprint planning for new AI/ML features
* Learn through mentorship, pair programming, and collaborative work with Sr. ML Architects and Data Scientists


**Who You Are:**

**Required Qualifications**:
* Currently pursuing or recently completed a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field with a focus on Machine Learning, AI, or Data Science
* Basic programming proficiency in Python
* Exposure to machine learning frameworks, algorithms, or concepts (e.g., NLP, data processing, classification)
* Strong willingness to learn and grow in a collaborative environment


**Preferred Qualifications:**


* Academic, internship, or personal project experience building data processing pipelines
* Familiarity with deep learning concepts, natural language processing (NLP), or anomaly detection techniques
* Exposure to AI development platforms such as AWS Bedrock / Sagemaker / Azure AI Foundry / Vertex AI or experience with AI Agents using Semantic Kernel or equivalent, Agentic AI, RAG, or Model fine\-tuning and/or MCP Servers
* Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure) or MLOps tools (CI/CD)
* Familiarity with Git and basic version control concepts
* Interest in working in SaaS data security or cybersecurity solutions


CrashPlan values workplace diversity and ensuring an environment of mutual respect. Employment opportunities are available to all applicants without regards to race, color, creed, religion, sex, national origin, age, marital status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, genetic information, or any other category protected by law. We believe that diversity and inclusion are critical to our success, and we seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

title: Senior Principal Consultant-AI / ML Solution
description:
Ready to build the future with AI?  

At Genpact, we don’t just keep up with technology—we set the pace. AI and digital innovation are redefining industries, and we’re leading the charge. Genpact’s AI Gigafactory, our industry\-first accelerator, is an example of how we’re scaling advanced technology solutions to help global enterprises work smarter, grow faster, and transform at scale. From large\-scale models to agentic AI, our breakthrough solutions tackle companies’ most complex challenges.  

If you thrive in a fast\-moving, innovation\-driven environment, love building and deploying cutting\-edge AI solutions, and want to push the boundaries of what’s possible, this is your moment.  

Genpact (NYSE: G) is an advanced technology services and solutions company that delivers lasting value for leading enterprises globally. Through our deep business knowledge, operational excellence, and cutting\-edge solutions – we help companies across industries get ahead and stay ahead. Powered by curiosity, courage, and innovation, our teams implement data, technology, and AI to create tomorrow, today. Get to know us at genpact.com and on LinkedIn, X, YouTube, and Facebook.  

Inviting applications for the role of Senior Principal Consultant\-AI / ML Solution Architect  

We are looking for a hands\-on AI/ML Solution Architect who can design, build, and scale real\-world AI systems—not just prototype models or create high\-level diagrams. This role sits at the intersection of business problems, data, and production\-grade AI engineering.  

You will be responsible for taking ambiguous business problems and translating them into end\-to\-end AI/ML solutions that are reliable, scalable, secure, and measurable in production..  

Responsibilities  

* Solution Architecture \& Design


o Own the end\-to\-end architecture of AI/ML solutions—from data ingestion to model serving and monitoring.  

o Design scalable, cloud\-native AI architectures using microservices, APIs, and event\-driven patterns.  

o Make technology trade\-offs across build vs buy, model selection, infrastructure, and tooling.  

* Hands\-On Model Development


o Build, train, fine\-tune, and evaluate ML / Deep Learning / GenAI models using Python  

o Work directly with Classical ML (classification, regression, anomaly detection), Deep learning (CNNs, Transformers) and LLMs (prompting, RAG, fine\-tuning, agents)  

o Translate PoCs into production\-ready models.  

* Data \& Feature Engineering


o Design data pipelines for structured, semi\-structured, and unstructured data.  

o Lead feature engineering, data validation, and data quality strategies.  

o Work closely with data engineers to ensure training–serving parity.  

* MLOps \& Productionization


o Build and own MLOps pipelines for Model versioning, CI/CD, Automated retraining, Model monitoring \& drift detection  

o Deploy models using Docker, Kubernetes, cloud ML platforms, or custom inference services.  

o Ensure solutions meet performance, scalability, security, and compliance requirements.  

* Stakeholder \& Technical Leadership


o Act as the technical authority for AI/ML initiatives.  

o Communicate clearly with products, engineering, leadership, and clients.  

o Mentor engineers and data scientists; set best practices and standards.  

Qualifications we seek in you!  

Minimum Qualifications  

* Experience in software engineering, data engineering, or ML engineering.
* Experience in building, designing, and deploying AI/ML systems in production.
* Strong programming skills in Python (mandatory); experience with Java/Scala is a plus.
* Proven experience with end\-to\-end ML lifecycle (data model deployment monitoring).
* Solid understanding of Supervised and unsupervised ML algorithms, Model evaluation and bias/variance trade\-offs,
* Hands\-on experience with PyTorch / TensorFlow, Scikit\-learn, LLM frameworks (LangChain, Langgraph, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, etc.)
* Solid experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, and embeddings.
* Strong experience with AWS / Azure / GCP (at least one).
* Experience with Kubernetes, Docker, REST / gRPC APIs, Streaming systems (Kafka, Pub/Sub)
* Familiarity with ML platforms (SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML).
* Experience with MLflow / Weights \& Biases, Feature stores, Model monitoring \& drift detection.
* Strong understanding of AI observability, reliability, and cost optimization
* Understanding of ethical and responsible AI


Preferred Qualifications/ Skills  

* Strong proficiency in C programming language with experience in developing
* Experience building AI platforms or reusable AI frameworks
* Experience with enterprise\-scale AI systems
* Strong opinion on AI safety, explainability, and responsible AI
* Ability to challenge requirements and simplify over\-engineered solutions


Why join Genpact?  

* Lead AI\-first transformation – Build and scale AI solutions that redefine industries
* Make an impact – Drive change for global enterprises and solve business challenges that matter
* Accelerate your career—Gain hands\-on experience, world\-class training, mentorship, and AI certifications to advance your skills
* Grow with the best – Learn from top engineers, data scientists, and AI experts in a dynamic, fast\-moving workplace
* Committed to ethical AI – Work in an environment where governance, transparency, and security are at the core of everything we build
* Thrive in a values\-driven culture – Our courage, curiosity, and incisiveness \- built on a foundation of integrity and inclusion \- allow your ideas to fuel progress


Come join the 140,000\+ coders, tech shapers, and growth makers at Genpact and take your career in the only direction that matters: Up.  

Let’s build tomorrow together.  

  

Genpact is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, religion or belief, sex, age, national origin, citizenship status, marital status, military/veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, physical or mental disability or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws. Genpact is committed to creating a dynamic work environment that values respect and integrity, customer focus, and innovation.  

Furthermore, please do note that Genpact does not charge fees to process job applications and applicants are not required to pay to participate in our hiring process in any other way. Examples of such scams include purchasing a 'starter kit,' paying to apply, or purchasing equipment or training.
